diagnostics.headline: wards 15 vs your 10 norm — above your level

players.name: Baldarast
players.experienceVerdict: low sample on this hero (outside the tracked pool)

flags: our damage is 4/5 physical — one enemy armor item blunts most of it

notes: Their main damage was The Fey (81k magical); 1/5 of you itemized magic resist — that hurt.
Enemy ran 3/5 on or near their meta core.

coaching.headline: Two Orb Primes taken, both wasted — a 47-minute loss with the structure war lost 6 to 14. You owned the prime pit and never cashed it into the map.
coaching.team: Trio of you (Goldilocks/Xeebs/Willy) on Murdock-carry, Bayle-offlane, Zinx-support, plus two randoms on TwinBlast mid and Grux jungle. The draft wasn't far off: jungle was favored (Grux 51.5% into Yin), support a touch unfavored (Zinx 50.4% into Riktor), and the three lanes only a hair behind — Murdock 48.4% into Sparrow, TwinBlast 48.5% into Fey, Bayle 47.6% into Steel. Roughly even on paper. You won the objective pit — mini-prime at 16, Orb Prime at 28 AND 42 — and still lost structures 6 to 14. That's the whole game: prime buffs that never became towers. Two things bled it out. First, Grux fed 9 deaths (norm 3) and TwinBlast 6 (norm 2), 15 between them, every one handing the map back while you held the buff. Second, damage profile: you're 4/5 physical, so one armor item blunts most of you, and only one of five bought magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You poked, they sieged.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: After Orb Prime the clock IS the buff — group five and break a tower lane immediately, don't go fish for picks into Riktor's chain-CC (pull→silence→suppress, ~2.5s) while it ticks away. Either of those two primes converted into structures wins this. And someone past Zinx needs a magic-resist item — Fey's 81k went unanswered. Anti-heal was fine; you brought two and Riktor's the only sustain, so don't spend the post-game on it.

perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Murdock carry, 7/5/2 — best damage on the team and the matchup into Sparrow read favored (48.4%), so this was yours to carry and you nearly did. Two fixes: 5 deaths vs your 3 norm means you got caught chasing out of position, and you skipped Lightning Hawk — the 53% core opener over 346 games. As the main threat on a 4/5-physical team that the enemy answers with a single armor piece, you need the crit/attack-speed spike from the core, not extra raw AD off Necrosis/Spear.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Bayle offlane, 4/5/6 — deaths in line with your norm, so no feeding here; the build is the miss. You ran Mutilator/Sky-Splitter/Tainted-Rounds/Salvation/Aegis and skipped the entire winning core (Overlord/Rapture/Cursed Scroll, 56% over 131). Bayle into Steel is already a hair behind (47.6%) and off-core makes it worse. Your Kingslayer knockback is half the team's hard CC — build to be the front that opens the fight on the tower, not a bruiser trading chip damage on the side.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Zinx support, 1/3/9 — your best line of the night: 9 assists, 432 mitigation/min (above your level), only 3 deaths. Nothing to fix in the play. Pick up Spectra to finish the core (Prophecy/Spectra/Orion, 52%). You did your job here — the loss was the team not converting the two primes into towers, not anything on your end.
perPlayer.3f5ff0dc-b979-43cf-adb6-9c0a794c1b6f: Grux jungle, 9/9/5 — don't let the kills flatter it. The matchup read favored (51.5% into Yin), so 9 deaths — triple the 3-death norm — was the play, not the draft. You stacked six damage items (Manta-Scythe, Terminus, Tainted-Trident, Deathstalker, Vanquisher) and skipped both Basilisk and every point of magic resist into a 3-magical comp with Fey on 81k. Every one of those deaths handed back the prime control the rest of the team kept winning. Build Basilisk, buy one magic resist, and stop feeding the magic burst.
perPlayer.f18f388a-9781-46b1-a864-934b1128cf3c: TwinBlast mid, 2/6/7 — the lowest-impact line on your side. The Fey matchup read unfavored (48.5%), but 6 deaths at triple the 2-death norm with only 2 kills is getting rolled in a lane that was close on paper. The build made it worse: you ran TWO anti-heal items (Mindrazor and Vanquisher) against a comp whose only healer is Riktor — a support, not a heal-stack — while skipping Ashbringer and Imperator, two-thirds of TwinBlast's winning core. Anti-heal isn't the priority when you're 2/6; surviving and hitting your power spike is. Build the core, drop the double anti-heal, stop dying for free.
